_comment: Words that the docs may name inside backticks that are NOT electron-deck exports. Each entry must be justified — a reviewer should be able to sanity-check why a documented symbol is allowed despite not appearing in the package export surface. Adding a name here is an explicit decision, not a silencer. See scripts/check-docs-api-sync.mjs.
_devtools_entry_symbols: Host-integration symbols the docs legitimately reference that are exported by @dimina-kit/devtools (not this package): launch() lives there to avoid a circular dep, and WorkbenchConfig/WorkbenchAppConfig are the devtools-side host config types.
_documented_as_removed: Old API name the docs mention only to say it no longer exists ('已被它取代、不复存在'). Kept so the historical note is allowed; the *current* drift name (createWorkbenchClient) is intentionally NOT here so substituting it still fails.
_ts_builtins_and_globals: TypeScript / JS built-ins and standard globals used in type positions inside code spans.
_electron_types: Electron runtime types referenced by the host-integration docs.
_electron_apis: Electron runtime methods/APIs called in example code, not electron-deck exports.
_host_example_types: Host-authored example type aliases in the docs' sample config (e.g. `export type HostServices = typeof hostServices`) — illustrative, defined by the downstream host, not exported by this package.
_brand_strings: App brand-name strings used in `app.name` examples, not symbols.
_devtools_internal_runtime: Implementation detail named in prose, not part of the electron-deck public surface.
_doc_local_type_params: Single/short type-parameter and example identifiers local to a code snippet (generics, example component names) — not package exports.
